articles.title: Batch Replace (Bulk Property Changes)
articles.whenToUse: When making bulk style changes across a subtree like rebranding or theme switching
articles.content:
### BATCH REPLACE (bulk property changes)

Use `grep` + `sed` for bulk style changes across a subtree (e.g., rebranding, theme switching):

**1. Discover current values** with `grep`:
```
grep /Card/ props
grep /Card/ fill,size,corner
```

**2. Replace** with precise from/to mappings using `sed`:
```
sed /Card/ fill:#3B82F6/#8B5CF6
sed /Card/ fill:#3B82F6/#8B5CF6 size:14/16 corner:8/12
sed /Card/ weight:Regular/Medium font:Inter/Helvetica
```

**Shorthand aliases** ‚ÄĒ use the same property names as `cat` output:
| Shorthand | Replaces |
|-----------|----------|
| `fill` | text color + frame fill |
| `bg` | frame fill only |
| `color` | text color only |
| `corner` | corner radius |
| `size` | font size |
| `weight` | font weight |
| `font` | font family |
| `stroke` | stroke color |
| `strokeW` | stroke weight |

All properties also accept their canonical names: `fillColor`, `textColor`, `cornerRadius`, `fontSize`, `fontFamily`, `fontWeight`, `strokeColor`, `strokeWeight`, `gap`, `opacity`.

articles.title: Canvas Reading (ls/tree/cat Progressive Pattern)
articles.whenToUse: When reading the canvas to understand what exists before making edits
articles.content:
## CANVAS READING (ls/tree/cat ‚ÄĒ Filesystem Pattern)

Three commands for reading the canvas, from broad to detailed. Path-based addressing: "/" for page root, "/NodeName/" for named nodes.

### ls(path) ‚ÄĒ "What's here?"
Lists direct children at a path. Shows name, type, dimensions, layout info.
Call this FIRST when you don't know what's on the canvas.
- `ls({path: "/"})` ‚ÄĒ page root children
- `ls({path: "/Card/"})` ‚ÄĒ Card's children

### tree(path, depth?) ‚ÄĒ "What's the structure?"
Structural tree: id, name, type, dimensions (w/h), layout mode. ~100-300 tokens.
Use for navigation, discovering children, planning edits.
Returns `suggestedReads` ‚ÄĒ paths of complex children worth inspecting with cat.
- `tree({path: "/Card/"})` ‚ÄĒ Card subtree
- `tree({path: "/", depth: 3})` ‚ÄĒ page structure, depth 3

### cat(path, depth?, screenshot?) ‚ÄĒ "What does it look like?"
Full styles: fills, fonts, effects, padding, cornerRadius, shadow, etc.
Auto-degrades to skeleton + hint when tree is large (>2500 chars).
Set screenshot=true to capture a visual screenshot.
- `cat({path: "/Card/Header/"})` ‚ÄĒ full Header properties
- `cat({path: "/Card/", screenshot: true})` ‚ÄĒ props + screenshot

### Progressive Reading Pattern
1. `ls({path: "/"})` ‚ÄĒ see page overview, find root node names
2. `tree({path: "/Card/"})` ‚ÄĒ discover structure, get suggestedReads
3. `cat({path: "/Card/Header/Title"})` ‚ÄĒ get full details for specific nodes
4. `mk /Card/Header/Title size:28` ‚ÄĒ edit based on detailed inspection

### Path Resolution
- "/" = current page root
- "/Card/" = child named "Card"
- "/Card/Header/Title" = nested path
- "Card#100:5" = name#id ref (from ls/receipt output ‚ÄĒ unique, O(1) lookup)

### XML Output Format (read tools)
tree and cat return compact XML. Note: read output is XML, write input is flat ops ‚ÄĒ they are different formats.

**Tag mapping**: FRAME‚Üí`<frame>`, TEXT‚Üí`<text>`, RECTANGLE‚Üí`<rect>`, VECTOR‚Üí`<vector>`, LINE‚Üí`<line>`, ELLIPSE‚Üí`<ellipse>`, GROUP‚Üí`<group>`, SECTION‚Üí`<section>`, ICON‚Üí`<icon>`

**Attribute abbreviations**:
- `layout` = layoutMode (V/H/NONE), `w` = width, `h` = height
- `sizingH` = layoutSizingHorizontal, `sizingV` = layoutSizingVertical
- `alignMain` = primaryAxisAlignItems, `alignCross` = counterAxisAlignItems
- `corner` = cornerRadius, `strokeW` = strokeWeight
- `p` = padding (compact: `p="16"` or `p="16 24"` or `p="10 20 30 40"`)
- `size` = fontSize, `weight` = fontWeight, `font` = fontFamily
- `fill` = single fill color, `fills` = multiple fill colors
- `shadow` = effects (format: `ox,oy,blur,spread,color`)

**Text content** appears as tag body: `<text size="16" weight="Bold" fill="#111827" textAutoResize="WIDTH_AND_HEIGHT">Hello World</text>`

**Example tree output** (structural skeleton):
```xml
<frame id="1:2" name="Card" layout="V" w="320" sizingV="HUG">
  <text id="3:4" name="Title">Welcome</text>
  <rect id="7:8" name="Divider" h="1"/>
</frame>
```

**Example cat output** (full styles):
```xml
<frame id="1:2" name="Card" layout="V" gap="12" fill="#FFF" w="320" sizingV="HUG" p="24">
  <text id="3:4" name="Title" size="24" weight="Bold" fill="#111" textAutoResize="WIDTH_AND_HEIGHT">Welcome</text>
  <rect id="7:8" name="Divider" h="1" fill="#E0E0E0"/>
</frame>
```

articles.title: Component-First Workflow
articles.whenToUse: When creating 2+ similar elements that share structure but differ in content
articles.content:
### COMPONENT-FIRST WORKFLOW (reusable elements)
When creating 2+ similar elements (cards, list items, nav items, stat tiles):

1. **Define once** ‚ÄĒ create a frame with all properties, then `comp create` to convert it to a Figma Component. Keep it small (3‚Äď8 nodes), include ALL design dimensions.
2. **Instantiate** ‚ÄĒ `comp instance` to stamp instances. Each instance inherits all styles.

```
mk /StatCard/ frame layout:column gap:8 p:20 bg:#FFFFFF corner:12 shadow:0,2,8,0,#0000001A w:240 h:hug
mk /StatCard/label text size:14 fill:#64748B -- Label
mk /StatCard/value text size:28 weight:Bold fill:#0F172A -- 0
comp create /StatCard/
```
Then:
```
mk /Stats/ frame layout:row gap:16 w:fill h:hug bg:transparent
comp instance /StatCard/ --parent /Stats/
comp instance /StatCard/ --parent /Stats/
comp instance /StatCard/ --parent /Stats/
```

**When to use**: 2+ similar elements with identical structure but different content.
**When NOT to use**: one-off layouts, unique sections ‚Üí direct `mk`.
**Key benefit**: component definition is small (focused attention = fewer attribute omissions), instances are tiny.

articles.title: Design System Tokens (Palette, Typography, Spacing)
articles.whenToUse: When creating or documenting a design system, extracting a palette from existing designs, or setting up visual tokens for a project
articles.content:
### DESIGN SYSTEM RENDER TOKENS

Use `render` with design system tokens to create visual token documentation.
Agent provides semantic content only ‚ÄĒ the runtime handles all layout and styling.

**Palette** ‚ÄĒ color swatches:
```
render({command: "render", input: "palette\n  swatch: Primary #000000\n  swatch: Secondary #475569\n  swatch: Accent #2D8BFF\n  swatch: Neutral #F8FAFC"})
```
Each `swatch: Name #HEX` generates: color block + name label + hex value.

**Typography** ‚ÄĒ font scale samples:
```
render({command: "render", input: "type-scale\n  type-sample: Headline Space-Grotesk 32 SemiBold\n  type-sample: Body Inter 16 Regular\n  type-sample: Caption Inter 12 Regular"})
```
Each `type-sample: Label FontFamily Size Weight` generates: label + rendered sample text.

**Spacing** ‚ÄĒ spacing scale visualization:
```
render({command: "render", input: "spacing-scale\n  spacing-step: xs 4\n  spacing-step: sm 8\n  spacing-step: md 16\n  spacing-step: lg 24"})
```
Each `spacing-step: Name Value` generates: label + proportional bar.

### WHEN TO USE
- User asks to "create a design system" or "set up a palette"
- You extracted colors/fonts from existing designs and want to document them
- User provides brand colors and wants a visual reference
- Starting a new project and establishing visual tokens

### WORKFLOW
1. Determine colors, fonts, spacing from user input or by inspecting existing canvas (`grep`, `cat`)
2. Render the design system sections using the tokens above
3. Tell the user what you created and how you'll use it in subsequent designs

articles.title: Error Catalog (Known Error Patterns)
articles.whenToUse: When debugging a tool error or unexpected behavior that might match a known pattern
articles.content:
# Error Catalog

Agent-facing error pattern registry. Check this file when debugging to see if the issue matches a known pattern.

Format: each entry is a pattern, not a one-off bug. Entries should help diagnose *future* similar issues.

---

## TYPE-001: Figma API ŤŅĒŚõěŚĮĻŤĪ°šĹÜ metadata Ś£įśėéšłļ scalar

**ÁóáÁä∂**: XML ŤĺďŚáļšł≠ŚáļÁéį `[object Object]`
**ś†ĻŚõ†**: `PROP_METADATA` ÁöĄ `type` Ś≠óśģĶšłé Figma API ŚģěťôÖŤŅĒŚõěÁĪĽŚěčšłćŚĆĻťÖć
**ŚŹĎÁéįśĖĻŚľŹ**: trigger result šł≠ grep `[object Object]`
**ŚĹĪŚďć**: read Ś∑•ŚÖ∑ŤŅĒŚõěŤĄŹśēįśćģ ‚Üí LLM ŚüļšļéťĒôŤĮĮšŅ°śĀĮŚĀöŚÜ≥Á≠Ė
**Ś∑≤Áü•Śģěšĺč**:
- `lineHeight` ‚ÄĒ Figma ŤŅĒŚõě `{unit: "PIXELS", value: 24}`ÔľĆmetadata Ś£įśėé `scalar`
- `letterSpacing` ‚ÄĒ Figma ŤŅĒŚõě `{unit: "PIXELS", value: 0}`ÔľĆmetadata Ś£įśėé `scalar`
**šŅģŚ§ć**: `propertyTransformer.ts` ŚĮĻ `{unit, value}` ŚĮĻŤĪ°śŹźŚŹĖ `.value`Ôľõ`xmlSerializer.ts` Śä† `typeof === 'object'` ťė≤Śĺ°
**ťė≤ÁļŅ**:
- [ ] PropertyTransformer Śä† runtime assertÔľąscalar ŤŅĒŚõě object śó∂ warnÔľČ
- [x] XmlSerializer ŤĺďŚáļŚĪā skip object values
**ś£ÄśĶčŤĄöśú¨**: `cat /tmp/figma-bridge/results/*/meta.json | grep -o '\[object Object\]' | wc -l`

---

## TRUNC-001: LLM tool call ŚŹāśēįŤĘęśą™śĖ≠ÔľĆXML šłćŚģĆśēī

**ÁóáÁä∂**: `XML_PARSE_ERROR: Unterminated element <text>`ÔľĆXML śúęŚįĺŚáļÁéį `‚Ä¶` (U+2026)
**ś†ĻŚõ†**: LLM ŤĺďŚáļ token Ť∂ÖťôźśąĖśĶĀŚľŹšľ†Ťĺďšł≠śĖ≠ÔľĆtool call ÁöĄ `xml` ŚŹāśēįŤĘęśą™śĖ≠šłļšłćŚģĆśēī XML
**ŚŹĎÁéįśĖĻŚľŹ**: trigger result šł≠śźú `Unterminated element`Ôľõśą™śĖ≠ XML ťēŅŚļ¶ŚľāŚłłÁü≠ÔľąŚ¶ā 201 chars vs ś≠£Śłł 600+ charsÔľČ
**ŚĹĪŚďć**: XML Ťß£śěźŚ§ĪŤī• ‚Üí ťĒôŤĮĮŤŅĒŚõě LLM ‚Üí LLM ťáćŤĮē ‚Üí ÁŅĽŚÄćŤÄóśó∂
**Ś§ćÁéįÁČĻŚĺĀ**:
- Kimi K2.5 100% Ś§ćÁéįÔľąšł§ś¨° trigger ŚģĆŚÖ®šłÄŤáīÁöĄśą™śĖ≠ÁāĻ„ÄĀŚģĆŚÖ®šłÄŤáīÁöĄ 4 šł™ tool callÔľČ
- śą™śĖ≠ŚŹĎÁĒüŚú®Á¨¨ 3 šł™ŚĻ∂Ť°Ć tool callÔľĆÁ¨¨ 1/2/4 šł™ś≠£ŚłłÔľąťĚěÁīĮÁßĮ token ťôźŚą∂ÔľČ
- XML śúęŚįĺŚł¶ `‚Ä¶` (U+2026)ÔľĆŤĮīśėéśėĮ LLM ŚÜÖťÉ®śą™śĖ≠ÔľąťĚěÁĹĎÁĽúšł≠śĖ≠ÔľČ
**šĽ£Á†ĀÁľļťô∑**: `agentRuntime.ts` ÁöĄ TRUNCATION GUARD Śú® `else` ŚąÜśĒĮÔľąśó† tool call śó∂śČćś£Äśü• `finishReason`ÔľČ„ÄāśúČ tool call śó∂Ść≥šĹŅ `finishReason=length`ÔľĆšĽćšľöśČßŤ°ĆŚ∑≤śą™śĖ≠ÁöĄ tool call
**ťė≤ÁļŅ**:
- [ ] Śú® tool dispatch ŚČćś£Äśü• `finishReason`ÔľĆŚ¶āśěúśėĮ `length` ŚąôšłĘŚľÉŚŹĮÁĖĎ tool call ŚĻ∂ś≥®ŚÖ•ÁĽ≠ŚÜôśŹźÁ§ļ
- [ ] XML parser ŚĪāÔľöś£ÄśĶč `‚Ä¶` ŚįĺŚ≠óÁ¨¶ÔľĆÁõīśé•ŤŅĒŚõě `TRUNCATED` ťĒôŤĮĮÁ†ĀÔľąŚĆļŚąęšļéŤĮ≠ś≥ēťĒôŤĮĮÔľČ
- [ ] ŤÄÉŤôĎ pre-dispatch XML ŚģĆśēīśÄßś†°ť™ĆÔľąťó≠Śźąś†áÁ≠ĺś£Äśü•ÔľČ
**ś£ÄśĶčŤĄöśú¨**: `python3 -c "..." # śźú trigger šł≠ xml śúęŚįĺŚźę U+2026 ÁöĄ tool call`

---

## TRUNC-002: LLM Śú® XML ŚĪěśÄßšł≠ŚÜôŚÖ• gradient JSONÔľĆÁ†īŚĚŹ XML Ťß£śěź

**ÁóáÁä∂**: `XML_PARSE_ERROR: Empty attribute name`ÔľõXML šł≠ŚáļÁéį `fills="[{\"type\":\"GRADIENT_LINEAR\",...}]"`
**ś†ĻŚõ†**: LLM šĽé read ÁĽďśěúŚ≠¶ŚąįšļÜ gradient ÁöĄ JSON ÁĽďśěĄÔľĆŚįĚŤĮēÁĒ® `\"` ŤĹ¨šĻČŚÜôŚÖ• XML ŚĪěśÄßÔľĆšĹÜ XML parser ŚįÜ `\"` ŤßÜšłļŚĪěśÄßŤĺĻÁēĆśĖ≠Ť£ā
**ŚŹĎÁéįśĖĻŚľŹ**: trigger result šł≠śźú `Empty attribute name`Ôľõś£Äśü• XML šł≠śėĮŚź¶Śźę escaped JSON
**ŚĹĪŚďć**: XML Ťß£śěźŚ§ĪŤī• ‚Üí LLM ťáćŤĮē 2-3 ś¨°ÔľąŚÖąÁĒ® `"` ŚÜć fallback Śąį solid colorÔľČ‚Üí ťĘĚŚ§Ė 2-3 šł™ iteration
**ťáćŤĮēś®°ŚľŹ** (ŤßāśĶčŚąįÁöĄ 3 ś¨°ŚįĚŤĮē):
1. `fills="[{\"type\":...}]"` ‚Üí Ś§ĪŤī•Ôľą`\"` Á†īŚĚŹ XMLÔľČ
2. `fills="[{\"type\":...}]"` ‚Üí ŚÜćś¨°Ś§ĪŤī•ÔľąŚźĆś†∑ťĒôŤĮĮÔľČ
3. `fills="[{"type":...}]"` ‚Üí śąźŚäüÔľąś≠£Á°ģ XML ŤĹ¨šĻČÔľČśąĖ `fill="#6366F1"` ‚Üí śąźŚäüÔľąśĒĺŚľÉ gradientÔľĆÁĒ® solidÔľČ
**ťė≤ÁļŅ**:
- [ ] XML parser ŚĪāÔľöś£ÄśĶč JSON-escaped quotes ś®°ŚľŹÔľĆŤŅĒŚõěśõīŚÖ∑šĹďÁöĄťĒôŤĮĮśŹźÁ§ļÔľąŚ¶ā "Use &quot; instead of \\" in XML attributes"ÔľČ
- [ ] read Ś∑•ŚÖ∑ŤŅĒŚõě gradient śó∂ÁĒ®ÁģÄŚĆĖś†ľŚľŹÔľąŚ¶ā `gradient="linear,#6366F1,#8B5CF6"`ÔľČŤÄĆťĚěŚéüŚßč JSON
- [ ] śąĖŚú® prompt šł≠śėéÁ°ģÁ¶Āś≠ĘŚú® XML ŚĪěśÄßšł≠ŚÜôŚÖ• JSON ŚĮĻŤĪ°

---

**ś†ĻŚõ†**: šĽ£Á†ĀŚĪāťĚĘšłļšĽÄšĻąŚŹĎÁĒü

articles.title: Error Handling (Escalation Strategy)
articles.whenToUse: When a tool call fails or returns an error code and you need to decide how to recover
articles.content:
## ERROR HANDLING (Escalation Strategy)
When a tool fails, escalate ‚ÄĒ don't loop:

| Failure count | Action |
|---|---|
| 1st | Use `cat /path/ -s` to diagnose. Fix the specific issue and retry with corrected parameters. |
| 2nd | Change approach ‚ÄĒ different structure, different parent, or simplified design. |
| 3rd+ | Complete with what you have. Explain the difficulty to the user in your completion text. |

Error codes:
- `NOT_FOUND` / `NO_MATCH`: The path doesn't exist. Use `ls /` or `tree /` to discover correct paths.
- `NOT_A_CONTAINER`: You tried to `ls` or add children to a leaf node (text, rectangle). Use `cat` to inspect it instead.
- `INVALID_TARGET`: Wrong node type for the operation (e.g., `comp ls` on a non-component).
- `exit:127`: Unknown command name. Check spelling ‚ÄĒ the system suggests closest matches.
- `{ retryTried: true }`: The engine exhausted auto-fixes. Do NOT micro-adjust. Either restructure fundamentally or complete and explain.

Warnings (e.g., `FONT_FALLBACK`): do NOT retry. Continue and mention it in your completion text.

articles.title: Tool Usage Examples
articles.whenToUse: When you need concrete examples of how to use design tools for various scenarios
articles.content:
## EXAMPLES

### Example 1: Simple component (single mk chain)
User: "Create a card with a title"

```
mk /Card/ frame layout:column gap:12 p:16 w:360 bg:#FFFFFF corner:12
mk /Card/Title text size:20 weight:Bold fill:#111827 w:fill -- Card Title
mk /Card/Subtitle text size:14 fill:#6B7280 w:fill -- Description text
```

### Example 2: Progressive creation (login page ‚ÄĒ grow step by step)
User: "Create a login page"

**Step 1 ‚ÄĒ Skeleton**: outer container + major sections as empty frames.
```
mk /Login_Page/ frame layout:column alignCross:center alignMain:center w:1440 h:900 bg:#F9FAFB
mk /Login_Page/Card frame layout:column gap:24 p:40 w:420 bg:#FFFFFF corner:16 shadow:0,4,24,0,#0000000F
mk /Login_Page/Card/Header frame layout:column gap:8 alignCross:center w:fill
mk /Login_Page/Card/Form frame layout:column gap:16 w:fill
mk /Login_Page/Card/Footer frame layout:column gap:12 alignCross:center w:fill
```

**Step 2 ‚ÄĒ Header region**: fill the header with logo and text.
```
mk /Login_Page/Card/Header/Logo text size:28 weight:Bold fill:#4F46E5 -- Acme
mk /Login_Page/Card/Header/Title text size:20 weight:Bold fill:#111827 -- Welcome back
mk /Login_Page/Card/Header/Subtitle text size:14 fill:#6B7280 -- Sign in to your account
```

**Step 3 ‚ÄĒ Form region**: email, password fields + button.
```
mk /Login_Page/Card/Form/EmailField frame layout:column gap:6 w:fill bg:transparent
mk /Login_Page/Card/Form/EmailField/Label text size:14 weight:Medium fill:#374151 -- Email
mk /Login_Page/Card/Form/EmailField/Input frame layout:row p:'12 16' w:fill bg:#FFFFFF corner:8 stroke:'1 #D1D5DB'
mk /Login_Page/Card/Form/EmailField/Input/Placeholder text size:14 fill:#9CA3AF -- you@example.com
mk /Login_Page/Card/Form/PasswordField frame layout:column gap:6 w:fill bg:transparent
mk /Login_Page/Card/Form/PasswordField/Label text size:14 weight:Medium fill:#374151 -- Password
mk /Login_Page/Card/Form/PasswordField/Input frame layout:row p:'12 16' w:fill bg:#FFFFFF corner:8 stroke:'1 #D1D5DB'
mk /Login_Page/Card/Form/PasswordField/Input/Placeholder text size:14 fill:#9CA3AF -- ‚ÄĘ‚ÄĘ‚ÄĘ‚ÄĘ‚ÄĘ‚ÄĘ‚ÄĘ‚ÄĘ
mk /Login_Page/Card/Form/SignInBtn frame layout:row alignMain:center alignCross:center p:12 w:fill h:44 bg:#4F46E5 corner:8
mk /Login_Page/Card/Form/SignInBtn/Label text size:16 weight:Bold fill:#FFFFFF -- Sign In
```

**Step 4 ‚ÄĒ Footer**: links and secondary text.
```
mk /Login_Page/Card/Footer/Forgot text size:14 fill:#4F46E5 -- Forgot password?
mk /Login_Page/Card/Footer/Signup text size:14 fill:#6B7280 -- Don't have an account? Sign up
```

**Step 5 ‚ÄĒ Verify + complete.**
```
cat /Login_Page/ -s
```
Check layout looks correct, then respond with text to complete.

### Example 3: Progressive read (ls ‚Üí tree ‚Üí cat)
User: "Update the header section in this complex page"

**Step 1 ‚ÄĒ Get canvas overview**:
```
ls /
```

**Step 2 ‚ÄĒ See the structure**:
```
tree /Dashboard/ -d 2
```

**Step 3 ‚ÄĒ Inspect the specific section**:
```
cat /Dashboard/Header/ -s
```

**Step 4 ‚ÄĒ Edit based on detailed inspection**:
```
mk /Dashboard/Header/Title fill:#4F46E5 size:24
```

### Example 4: Query-first edit
User: "Change the button in the existing card to green and add rounded corners"

```
tree /Card/ -d 2
mk /Card/Button bg:#10B981 corner:10
```

### Example 5: Batch replace (rebranding)
User: "Change all blue (#3B82F6) to purple (#8B5CF6) in the card"

```
grep /Card/ fill,stroke
sed /Card/ fill:#3B82F6/#8B5CF6 stroke:#3B82F6/#8B5CF6
```

### Example 6: Component-first card row (comp create + instance)
User: "Create a stats dashboard with 3 metric cards"

**Step 1 ‚ÄĒ Define the reusable component** (small, all attributes):
```
mk /StatCard/ frame layout:column gap:8 p:20 bg:#FFFFFF corner:12 shadow:0,2,8,0,#0000001A w:240 h:hug
mk /StatCard/label text size:14 fill:#64748B -- Label
mk /StatCard/value text size:28 weight:Bold fill:#0F172A -- 0
comp create /StatCard/
```

**Step 2 ‚ÄĒ Stamp instances with overrides**:
```
mk /Stats/ frame layout:row gap:16 w:fill h:hug bg:transparent
comp instance /StatCard/ --parent /Stats/
comp instance /StatCard/ --parent /Stats/
comp instance /StatCard/ --parent /Stats/
```

### Example 7: Variant ComponentSet with clone (multi-axis)
User: "Create a Button component with Primary/Neutral variants and Default/Hover states"

**Step 1 ‚ÄĒ Base component**:
```
mk /Base/ frame layout:row gap:8 p:12 corner:8 bg:#2C2C2C stroke:'1 #2C2C2C' w:hug h:hug alignMain:center alignCross:center overflow:hidden
mk /Base/Label text size:16 fill:#F5F5F5 -- Button
```

**Step 2 ‚ÄĒ Clone variants** (only differences):
```
cp /Base/ /Hover/ bg:#1E1E1E
cp /Base/ /Disabled/ bg:#D9D9D9 stroke:'1 #B3B3B3'
cp /Base/ /Neutral/ bg:#E3E3E3 stroke:'1 #767676'
```

**Step 3 ‚ÄĒ Combine into ComponentSet**:
```
comp combine /Base/ /Hover/ /Disabled/ /Neutral/ --name Button
```

### Example 8: Completion (text-only response, no tool calls)
After all design work is done, respond with text only ‚ÄĒ this ends the turn:

"I've created the login form with email/password fields, a sign-in button, and proper card styling. The form uses vertical auto-layout with 16px spacing."

Note: Every frame has explicit `bg` ‚ÄĒ structural frames use `bg:transparent` so they don't override the parent's dark background.

articles.title: Modification (Update and Delete Operations)
articles.whenToUse: When modifying, updating, or deleting existing nodes on the canvas
articles.content:
### MODIFICATION (update and delete operations)

**Update existing nodes** ‚ÄĒ `mk` on an existing path updates properties (upsert):
```
mk /Card/ corner:16 bg:#F3F4F6
mk /Card/Title fill:#EF4444 size:18
```
Only listed properties change; everything else is preserved.

**Delete nodes** ‚ÄĒ `rm` removes a node and its children:
```
rm /Card/OldSection/
rm /Card/Temp*          ‚Üź glob: delete all children starting with "Temp"
```
Safety: `rm` warns when deleting nodes you didn't create (`‚ö† not created by you`).
If you see this warning and the user didn't explicitly ask for deletion, stop and confirm:
"Found existing [node name] ‚ÄĒ should I remove it or work alongside it?"

**CRITICAL: target existing nodes by path (from ls/tree output) or by name#id ref (`Card#100:5`).**

**BATCH EDITS**: Use `sed` for bulk property changes across a subtree ‚ÄĒ one command replaces all matching values:
```
sed /Card/ fill:#3B82F6/#8B5CF6 size:14/16 corner:8/12
```

**Mixed create + edit** (chain with &&):
```
mk /Card/NewLabel text size:14 fill:#6B7280 -- Added text && rm /Card/OldLabel/
```

articles.title: Parent-Child Creation
articles.whenToUse: When inserting children into existing frames or using idMap from previous design calls
articles.content:
## PARENT-CHILD CREATION

**Progressive building** ‚ÄĒ create skeleton first, then add children:
```
mk /Card/ frame w:400 layout:column p:24 bg:#FFF corner:12
mk /Card/Header frame layout:row alignCross:center gap:12
mk /Card/Header/Title text size:18 weight:Bold fill:#111 -- Card Title
mk /Card/Body text size:14 fill:#666 -- Description here
```

**Insert into existing trees** ‚ÄĒ use `tree` or `ls` first to discover the structure, then `mk` into the path:
```
tree /ExistingCard/ -d 2
mk /ExistingCard/Footer frame layout:row gap:8 alignMain:end
mk /ExistingCard/Footer/Btn frame layout:row p:'8 16' bg:#000 corner:6
mk /ExistingCard/Footer/Btn/Label text size:12 fill:#FFF weight:Medium -- Action
```

**$LAST shortcut** ‚ÄĒ reference the last created node in chains:
```
mk /Card/Icon frame w:40 h:40 bg:#EEE corner:20 && cat $LAST -s
```

**By name#id** ‚ÄĒ use ref from ls/receipt for precise targeting:
```
mk /NewChild#1134:25994/ text size:14 fill:#333 -- Inserted by ref
```

articles.title: Progressive Creation Strategy
articles.whenToUse: When deciding how many mk calls to use based on design complexity
articles.content:
### PROGRESSIVE CREATION (grow the design step by step)
Scale your approach to the design's complexity:

| Complexity | Node count | Strategy |
|---|---|---|
| **Simple** (card, button, form) | ‚Č§15 nodes | **Sequential mk calls** ‚ÄĒ create ALL nodes with full attributes. No skeleton step needed. |
| **Medium** (login page, settings panel) | 15‚Äď40 nodes | **2‚Äď3 rounds** ‚ÄĒ skeleton + fill regions. Each round ~10‚Äď15 nodes. |
| **Complex** (dashboard, multi-section page) | 40+ nodes | **4+ rounds** ‚ÄĒ progressive rhythm below. |

For medium/complex designs, break creation into semantic steps:
1. **Skeleton** ‚ÄĒ outer container + major layout sections (empty frames with names, sizing, bg)
2. **Region by region** ‚ÄĒ fill each section with its content (one round of `mk` per logical area)
3. **Details** ‚ÄĒ icons, decorative elements, shadows, polish
4. **Verify** ‚ÄĒ `cat /path/ -s` to check the result, `mk` to fix issues

**IMPORTANT**: Each round should create **5‚Äď15 nodes**. Do NOT split into rounds with only 1‚Äď3 nodes ‚ÄĒ that wastes iterations. Pack as many related nodes as possible into each round.

> **Modification**: If asked to "modify", "update", "fix", or "add to" an existing design, use `mk /path/ props` to update existing nodes (upsert), `rm /path/` to delete, or `sed /path/` for bulk changes.

articles.title: Property Reference (mk command)
articles.whenToUse: When you need to know available property names for mk create/update commands
articles.content:
### PROPERTY REFERENCE

Properties use `key:value` syntax in mk commands. Both shorthand and canonical names are accepted.

#### Layout
| Shorthand | Canonical | Values |
|-----------|-----------|--------|
| `layout` | layoutMode | `row`, `column`, `none`, `grid` (with `cols:N` ‚ÄĒ auto-generates equal child frames) |
| `pattern` | (composite) | `row`, `column`, `row-fill`, `column-fill`, `stack` |
| `align` | primary+counterAxisAlignItems | `center`, `start`, `end`, `space-between` |
| `alignMain` | primaryAxisAlignItems | `center`, `start`, `end`, `space-between` |
| `alignCross` | counterAxisAlignItems | `center`, `start`, `end`, `baseline` |
| `gap` | itemSpacing | number |
| `crossGap` | counterAxisSpacing | number |
| `wrap` | layoutWrap | `wrap`, `nowrap` |
| `positioning` | layoutPositioning | `auto`, `absolute` |

#### Sizing
| Shorthand | Canonical | Values |
|-----------|-----------|--------|
| `w` | width | number, `fill`, `hug` |
| `h` | height | number, `fill`, `hug` |
| `sizing` | layoutSizingH+V | `HUG`, `FILL`, `FIXED` |
| `sizingH` | layoutSizingHorizontal | `HUG`, `FILL`, `FIXED` |
| `sizingV` | layoutSizingVertical | `HUG`, `FILL`, `FIXED` |
| `minW` | minWidth | number |
| `maxW` | maxWidth | number |
| `minH` | minHeight | number |
| `maxH` | maxHeight | number |
| `lockRatio` | constrainProportions | `true`, `false` |

#### Spacing
| Shorthand | Canonical | Values |
|-----------|-----------|--------|
| `p` | padding (all sides) | number or `top right bottom left` |
| `pt` | paddingTop | number |
| `pr` | paddingRight | number |
| `pb` | paddingBottom | number |
| `pl` | paddingLeft | number |

#### Appearance
| Shorthand | Canonical | Values |
|-----------|-----------|--------|
| `fill` / `bg` | fills | `#hex`, `transparent`, `none`, `linear-gradient(deg, #color, #color)`, `radial-gradient(...)`, `diamond-gradient(...)` |
| `stroke` | strokes+strokeWeight+strokeAlign | `#hex width align` |
| `outline` | strokes+strokeAlign:OUTSIDE | `#hex weight` ‚ÄĒ for focus rings. Add `outlineOffset:N` for gap |
| `strokeW` | strokeWeight | number |
| `strokeA` | strokeAlign | `inside`, `outside`, `center` |
| `strokeJ` | strokeJoin | `miter`, `bevel`, `round` |
| `strokeC` | strokeCap | `none`, `round`, `square` |
| `dash` | dashPattern | array e.g. `[10,5]` |
| `strokeT/R/B/L` | strokeTop/Right/Bottom/LeftWeight | number |
| `opacity` | opacity | 0-1 |
| `blend` | blendMode | `normal`, `multiply`, `screen`, etc. |
| `visible` | visible | `true`, `false` |

#### Shape
| Shorthand | Canonical | Values |
|-----------|-----------|--------|
| `corner` / `radius` | cornerRadius | number, `full`, `[tl,tr,bl,br]` |
| `smooth` | cornerSmoothing | 0-1 |
| `overflow` | clipsContent | `hidden`/`clip`=true, `visible`=false |

#### Effects
| Shorthand | Canonical | Values |
|-----------|-----------|--------|
| `shadow` | effects | `"x y blur spread #color"` |
| `blur` | effects (layer blur) | number (radius) |
| `bgblur` | effects (bg blur) | number (radius) |

#### Text
| Shorthand | Canonical | Values |
|-----------|-----------|--------|
| `size` | fontSize | number |
| `weight` | fontWeight | `thin`, `light`, `regular`, `medium`, `semi-bold`, `bold`, `extra-bold`, `black`, or numeric 100-900 |
| `font` | fontFamily | string (supports `$variable` binding) |
| `italic` | fontStyle | `true`, `false` ‚ÄĒ combines with weight to build style string |
| `slant` | fontSlant | number (variable font oblique axis) |
| `decoration` | textDecoration | `underline`, `strikethrough`, `none` |
| `truncate` | textTruncation+textAutoResize | `true` ‚ÄĒ enables ellipsis. NEVER write literal "..." |
| `maxLines` | maxLines | number ‚ÄĒ auto-enables truncation. Use with `truncate:true` |
| `whiteSpace` | textAutoResize | `nowrap`=WIDTH_AND_HEIGHT, `normal`=HEIGHT |
| `textAlign` | textAlignHorizontal | `left`, `center`, `right`, `justified` |
| `leading` / `lineHeight` | lineHeight | number or `%` (values <=5 treated as multiplier) |
| `tracking` | letterSpacing | number |
| `characters` | characters | text content (or use `-- text` syntax) |
| `textCase` | textCase | `UPPER`, `LOWER`, `TITLE`, `ORIGINAL` |

#### Layout Details
| Shorthand | Canonical | Values |
|-----------|-----------|--------|
| `x` | x | number (position) |
| `y` | y | number (position) |
| `rotate` | rotation | number (CSS clockwise degrees, auto-negated for Figma) |
| `rotation` | rotation | number (degrees, Figma native counter-clockwise) |
| `pin` | constraints | `{horizontal, vertical}` |
| `strokesInLayout` | strokesIncludedInLayout | `true`, `false` |
| `reverseZ` | itemReverseZIndex | `true`, `false` |
| `layoutGrow` | layoutGrow | 0 or 1 |
| `layoutAlign` | layoutAlign | `STRETCH`, `INHERIT` |

#### Icon
| Shorthand | Canonical | Values |
|-----------|-----------|--------|
| `iconName` | iconName | `prefix:name` ‚ÄĒ Iconify API format |
| `w` | width | number (default 24) |
| `h` | height | number (default 24) |
| `fill` | fills | `#hex` ‚ÄĒ recolors all icon vectors |

**Prefixes**: `lucide`, `mdi`, `heroicons`, `tabler`, `f7`, `hugeicons`, `logos` (brand icons with original colors).
**Example**: `mk /Card/Icon icon iconName:lucide:heart w:20 h:20 fill:#EF4444`

#### Image
| Shorthand | Canonical | Values |
|-----------|-----------|--------|
| `fit` | scaleMode (IMAGE paint) | `cover`=FILL, `contain`=FIT, `none`=CROP, `tile`=TILE |

#### Grid (macro)
`layout:grid cols:3 gap:16` expands into a row frame with N equal-width (`w:fill`) child frames.
Mixed widths: `cols:"200 1fr 300"` ‚Üí fixed + fill + fixed children.

#### Variable Binding
Any property value starting with `$` binds to a Figma variable: `fill:$colors/primary`
Gradient stops also support variables: `fill:linear-gradient(90deg, $colors/primary, $colors/secondary)`

articles.title: Response Style Guide (CLI, not chatbot)
articles.whenToUse: When you need guidance on how to format your text responses
articles.content:
## RESPONSE STYLE (CLI, not chatbot)

Your text output follows Unix philosophy: terse, information-dense, outcome-focused.

### Format by scenario

| Scenario | Format | Example |
|----------|--------|---------|
| Completion | 1 line: what + dimensions | `‚úď Landing page: Hero + Features + CTA (1280√ó3200)` |
| Clarification | 1 question | `Dark or light theme?` |
| Failure | Error + tried + suggestion, 2-3 lines | `‚úó icon "lucide:xyz" not found. Tried: lucide:arrow-right (ok). Suggest: check icon name.` |
| Modification | 1 line: what changed | `‚úď Updated: Card corner 8‚Üí16, shadow added` |

### Anti-patterns (NEVER do these)

```
‚ĚĆ ŤģĺŤģ°ŚģĆśąźÔľĀŤģ©śąĎśÄĽÁĽďšłÄšłčŚąõŚĽļÁöĄŚÜÖŚģĻÔľö
‚ĚĆ ## Claudeť£éś†ľLanding Page ŤģĺŤģ°ŚģĆśąź ‚ú®
‚ĚĆ śąĎšłļśā®ŚąõŚĽļšļÜšłÄšł™ŚģĆśēīÁöĄLanding PageÔľĆŚĆÖŚźęšĽ•šłčÁČĻÁāĻÔľö
‚ĚĆ ### ūüé® ŤģĺŤģ°ť£éś†ľ ...
```

These waste output tokens, context tokens, and user attention.

### The rule

Every word in your response must carry information. If the user can see the design on canvas, don't describe it ‚ÄĒ just confirm it's done.

articles.title: Style Guide for Visual Direction
articles.whenToUse: When creating a new design from scratch and needing a consistent visual direction or color/type system
articles.content:
### STYLE GUIDE FOR VISUAL DIRECTION
When creating a NEW design from scratch (not editing existing), use style guides only when they add signal:
1. If the request does not already imply a clear visual direction, or you want a bundled palette/type system, optionally call `man style-tags`
2. Pick 2-4 specific tags that capture use case first, then mode/accent/mood
3. `man style dark-mode,dashboard,blue-accent` ‚ÄĒ get color/font/spacing system
4. Apply the style guide's color tokens, typography, spacing, and shape values to your `mk` calls
5. Skip style queries when the user already specified the look, or when matching an existing canvas/design system matters more than exploration

articles.title: Clone for Variant Matrices
articles.whenToUse: When creating a ComponentSet with multiple variants across axes like style, state, and size
articles.content:
### CLONE FOR VARIANT MATRICES
When creating a ComponentSet with many variants (e.g. Button √ó 3 styles √ó 3 states √ó 2 sizes = 18 variants):

1. **Define one base** ‚ÄĒ create a frame with ALL properties (the "source of truth")
2. **Clone variants** ‚ÄĒ `cp /Base/ /VariantName/` with only differences as overrides
3. **Cascade** ‚ÄĒ clone from a clone to layer overrides (e.g. Small = clone from Medium with different padding)
4. **Combine** ‚ÄĒ `comp combine /Base/ /V2/ /V3/ --name ComponentName`

```
mk /Base/ frame layout:row gap:8 p:12 corner:8 bg:#2C2C2C stroke:'1 #2C2C2C' w:hug h:hug alignMain:center alignCross:center overflow:hidden
mk /Base/Label text size:16 fill:#F5F5F5 -- Button
cp /Base/ /Hover/ bg:#1E1E1E
cp /Base/ /Disabled/ bg:#D9D9D9 stroke:'1 #B3B3B3'
cp /Base/ /Small/ p:8
comp combine /Base/ /Hover/ /Disabled/ /Small/ --name Button
```

**Key benefit**: 18 variants in ~20 lines instead of ~36. Each clone specifies ONLY differences ‚Üí minimal omission risk.
